pieces.title: Gloom
pieces.description: One of my first ever pieces! Done for a daily theme.

pieces.title: Buried Treasure
pieces.description: Buried treasure, done for a daily theme.

pieces.title: Aurora Borealis
pieces.description: An aurora done for a daily theme. I think this was my first time using the blur feature.

pieces.title: Tiny Samurai
pieces.description: I pretty much copied this exactly from Brandom James Greer, though at the time I had no idea who he was, or that he'd turn into one of my biggest pixel art inspirations.

pieces.title: Christmas Tree
pieces.description: This was done for a daily challenge for the holidays.

pieces.title: Jingle Bells
pieces.description: Another holiday challenge. I remember being blown away by how dimensional this looked and how cool the specular highlight looked.

pieces.title: Houseplants
pieces.description: A cute little cactus for a daily theme! I remember that I used a video game screenshot for the reference, but can't remember which one :(

pieces.title: Peacock
pieces.description: I remember being really happy with how the peacock turned out, but so confused as to what to do for a background haha.

pieces.title: Sunflower
pieces.description: I was SO proud of this one, I changed all my profile pictures to this one and neverr went back!

pieces.title: Charmander
pieces.description: This was one one of my first big projects. There was a #PixelDailies theme that I created one Charmander for, and the rest followed!

pieces.title: Potion's Class
pieces.description: Another big project! I had to look up a few tutorials for this. Small sprites, and isometric art. I think I had just finished one of the first few Harry Potter books, which inspired this.

pieces.title: Tongue (Puss in Boots)
pieces.description: Other than actual projects, this was one of the first pieces where I started using a canvas bigger than 32x32. The #PixelDailies theme was tongue, so I grabbed a reference from Puss in Boots sticking out his lil cat tongue :)

pieces.title: Gengar
pieces.description: I saw an amazing Gengar expression sheet (by 3buddy), and had to try to recreate it in pixel art.

pieces.title: Silly Dog Tongue
pieces.description: My girlfriend's dog has a habit of sticking her tongue out randomly, so I decided to turn it into pixel art!

pieces.title: Dachsusnd on a Dachsund Statue
pieces.description: I found a cute reference of a dachsund stgagndong on top of a dachsund statue, so I tried to my best to make it in a super low-res :)

pieces.title: 1-bit Baldur's Gate 3
pieces.description: I was late to Baldur's Gate 3, but I put in like 100 hours in the first few weeks. I wanted to make some art inspired by it, and I was learning both 1-bit art and portraits, so I thought why not combine them?

pieces.title: Earthworm
pieces.description: This was done for a #pixeldailies challenge and I remember being so impressed by how the specular highlights looked when it was completed.

pieces.title: Severance imagined as a GBA game
pieces.description: I was ADDICTED to Severance when season 2 came out. I had to make some art inspired by it, and this was probably my most ambitious project up to this point. I remember spending so long on the Lumon logo, the curves were just so hard to get right.

pieces.title: Squirtle
pieces.description: I think I did the crying squirtle first for a #pixeldailies challenge, and made the rest of this on a long train ride the weekend after.

pieces.title: Kirby
pieces.description: I made this piece a few different times, and unfortunately I overwrote it each time instead of exporting it as a new piece. I left is in this resolution, which was an experiment to see how Instagram worked, since I had just made one.

pieces.title: Dragonite eating a hamburger
pieces.description: I used one of the default palettes on Aseprite for this #pixeldailies challenge (the theme was emotion).

pieces.title: Vegetable Garden Stamps
pieces.description: My girlfriend got into gardening during the summer of 2024 so I wanted to make her some cute veggie stamps!

pieces.title: Obsidian
pieces.description: The #pixeldailies theme was "scintillating" which I had no idea what it meant until I looked it up (sparkling or shining brightly). This was a great introduction to how light is reflected on/off of/inside of gems and other translucent materials. So much fun!

pieces.title: 1-bit Severance Portraits
pieces.description: Taking inspiration from my earlier 1-bit portraits from Baldur's Gate 3, I wanted to attempt some more 1-bit portraits of the main characters from Severance.

pieces.title: Bulbasaur
pieces.description: I think this was the first piece I had done with a deliberately created palette. I wanted to keep the color count low, and it was tough getting the red to look right. It still feels off to me haha.

pieces.title: Simple Tomato
pieces.description: This was the first tomato that would eventually become my garden vegetable stamps, done for my girlfriend in November 2024.

pieces.title: 1-bit Capybara
pieces.description: For August 2024, I challenged myself to do the #PixelDailies challenge in 1-bit!

pieces.title: 1-bit Wabi-sabi
pieces.description: Wabi-sabi is a Japanese aesthetic philosphy which centers on the acceptance of transience and imperfection (per Wikipedia).

pieces.title: 2-bit Tea Kettle
pieces.description: For September 2024, I challenged myself to use the same 2-bit palette for every piece.

pieces.title: 2-bit Guts
pieces.description: Side profile of Guts from Berserk.

pieces.title: 2-bit Princess Peach
pieces.description: The Princess Peach challenge was HUGE around this time, so I wanted to give it a go with the 2-bit palette I was committed to using for September.

pieces.title: 2-bit Harry Poter
pieces.description: I'm not super happy with this, but I think it's important to show work you're not always the most proud of.

pieces.title: 2-bit Ameleia Earhart
pieces.description: I'm SO HAPPY with how this turned out. I wanted to try to stylize a reference and think I did a really good job!

pieces.title: Boulder (From Avatar: The Last Airbender)
pieces.description: One of my favorite side characters from Avatar!

pieces.title: Doot Doot
pieces.description: I think the #PixelDailies challenge was Skeleton, so I had to pay tribute to a classic meme.

pieces.title: Game Jam mock-up
pieces.description: I can't quite remember the context of this, other than that it was a submission for a game jam (Mini-Jam 171). I think I used the PICO-8 palette, but I can't remember if I submitted this as a mock-up or as part of a team.

pieces.title: Piranha Plant
pieces.description: When I made this Piranha Plant, I was just starting to understand hue shifting and wasn't sure about how this looked. Now (in March of 2026) I am looking back on this and I'm so impressed!

pieces.title: Coffee
pieces.description: After sticking with 32x32 for a few months, I wanted to move onto a bigger resolution, so for this #PixelDailies theme I tried my hand at a 64x64 coffee cup.

pieces.title: Bunny in a hat
pieces.description: This was a halloween #PixelDailies challenge (the theme was trick), and I wasn't sure what to do, so I settled on the classic magician trick.

pieces.title: 1-bit Vampire Castles
pieces.description: I think I was rewatching Castlevania (on Netflix) around this time and was obsessed with how Dracula's castle looked, so I wanted to do some castle studies with a "dracula"-esque 1-bit palette.

pieces.title: Dandelion Kirby
pieces.description: What would Kirby look like if he had dandelion powers?

pieces.title: Cheesy Slice
pieces.description: I can't remember if this was a #PixelDailies challenge or if I just really wanted pizza at the time.

pieces.title: Balsom Root Flower
pieces.description: I wanted to make a series of pixel art flowers for my girlfriend, but I think I made just this one and left the project incomplete 😢
